title: Reimburse Ikigai Info Governance Action Deposit.

rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The submitter of the September 2024 'Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -' Info governance action was unable to recover a 100,000 ADA deposit because of a Cardano node bug involving an unregistered stake key." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury, consisting of the original 100,000 ADA deposit plus 3,000 ADA for estimated lost staking rewards." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal states that the recipient has not previously received funds from the treasury; Provide budget analysis. Residual uncertainty: Treasury diligence dossier incomplete; soft gate applied a -0.1 caution penalty and judged on available repo context (anchor + doctrine + reasoning lean) rather than abstaining. Confidence context: score -0.48; confidence 64.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: DRep ratification support is material but below threshold; treated as a modest context signal.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The proposal states that the recipient has not previously received funds from the treasury.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to reimburse the lost deposit for the Ikigai Info governance action and compensate estimated lost staking rewards.
Recipient: Ikigai Info governance action submitter
Deliverables: Immediate on-chain distribution of funds to the intended recipient upon enactment
Deadline/expiry: instantly performed as part of the governance action's enactment on-chain

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury, consisting of the original 100,000 ADA deposit plus 3,000 ADA for estimated lost staking rewards.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The submitter of the September 2024 'Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -' Info governance action was unable to recover a 100,000 ADA deposit because of a Cardano node bug involving an unregistered stake key.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, medium materiality): The referenced Ikigai governance action was symbolic and asked the community whether they agreed with a statement of thanks and hope for Cardano's future.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer states there was significant community sentiment that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed because the submitter was an early pioneer of Cardano governance.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in a budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal states that the recipient has not previously received funds from the treasury.
missing: Independent evidence for: The submitter of the September 2024 'Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -' Info governance action was unable to recover a 100,000 ADA deposit because of a Cardano node bug involving an unregistered stake key.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states there was significant community sentiment that the Ikigai Info governance action deposit should be reimbursed because the submitter was an early pioneer of Cardano governance.
Independent evidence for: The Cardano in Oceania initiative included this reimbursement in a budget Info governance action, but that governance action was not approved.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that the recipient has not previously received funds from the treasury.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unsustainable (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 103,000 ADA from the treasury, consisting of the original 100,000 ADA deposit plus 3,000 ADA for estimated lost staking rewards." though 5 review blocker(s) remain open.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The submitter of the September 2024 'Cardanoの生きがい - Ikigai -' Info governance action was unable to recover a 100,000 ADA deposit because of a Cardano node bug involving an unregistered stake key." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
